methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series G infrastructure and logistics frontier-tech company founded by Roman Teslyuk, presents a limited comparable universe for valuation. The broader sector contains only one identified private peer (AiDash) and no public comparables, constraining traditional peer-median approaches.

Of the three triangulation methods evaluated, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method anchors to Earth AI's $20M total raised and derives a $75M proxy base value, then applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G progression, yielding $94M. The peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in Infrastructure & Logistics—the single sector peer identified did not meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $80M (low) to $108M (high), centered on $94M. This median reflects stage-appropriate capital deployment multiples but carries material uncertainty given the sparse peer set. Key risks to valuation stability include: (1) the absence of public market anchors in this sector, (2) reliance on a single methodology rather than triangulated methods, and (3) limited visibility into Earth AI's unit economics, revenue trajectory, or competitive positioning relative to the one identified peer. A deeper assessment of stage G fundamentals and customer concentration would strengthen conviction in this range.
